channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: BFAR Issues Warning on Poisonous Fish and Crabs That Can Be Fatal
item.description: The Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources (BFAR) reminded the public to avoid eating certain fish and crab species due to the presence of deadly toxins. Specifically, BFAR identified the Pufferfis...

item.title: Prince Albert toddler dies; police and coroner investigating
item.description: Police in Prince Albert, Saskatchewan, are conducting an investigation into the death of a three-year-old boy. On the evening of March 3, officers responded to a residence in the 900 Block of 2nd Stre...

item.title: Municipal Licensing Delays Hinder Spaza Shops from Receiving Government Support
item.description: The Portfolio Committee on Small Business Development has highlighted that delays by municipalities in issuing operating licences are significantly hindering spaza shops from accessing government fund...

item.title: SBP Expected to Maintain Policy Rate at 10.5% Amid Rising Oil Prices and Inflation Risks
item.description: The State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) is widely expected to keep its key policy rate unchanged at 10.5 per cent during the upcoming monetary policy review on Monday, according to a Reuters poll of analysts...

item.title: Rivers State Launches Dr Peter Odili Cancer and Cardiovascular Centre with Free Screening Services
item.description: The Rivers State Government don officially open the Dr Peter Odili Cancer and Cardiovascular Centre for public use inside Port Harcourt. The centre dey offer free screening for cancer plus different c...

item.title: John Crace skewers Kemi Badenoch's chaotic Today programme interview on Iran conflict
item.description: In his signature satirical style, Guardian columnist John Crace delivers a scathing critique of Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch's appearance on BBC Radio 4's Today programme with Nick Robinson on 5 ...

item.title: Kenya Airways Appoints New Chairman and Board Members
item.description: Kenya Airways (KQ) has recently announced major changes in its board leadership. Kiprono Kittony has been named as the new Chairman, taking over from Michael Joseph who retired from the role. Kittony ...

item.title: Pakistan Government Mulls Remote Work, Online Classes and Reduced Office Attendance to Conserve Energy Amid Rising Oil Prices
item.description: The federal government of Pakistan is seriously looking into several energy-saving steps under a proposed national action plan, as the country faces possible fuel shortages due to rising international...

item.title: Pakistan urges restraint and diplomacy over attacks in Turkiye and Azerbaijan
item.description: Pakistan has voiced serious concern over the recent attacks targeting its close allies Turkiye and Azerbaijan amid the ongoing Middle East conflict involving Iran, the US, and Israel. On Thursday, a d...

item.title: Crystal Palace Manager Oliver Glasner Gets Six-Month Driving Ban for Speeding in London
item.description: The oga wey dey manage Crystal Palace, Oliver Glasner, don get him driving licence suspend for six months after dem catch am dey drive pass the speed limit for London. Na speed camera catch am dey wak...

item.title: Popular adult websites restrict Australian access ahead of mandatory age checks
item.description: As of early March 2026, several major porn websites have started blocking Australian users entirely, ahead of new government-mandated age verification rules. From March 9, Australian law will require ...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens Environmental Protection and Climate Change Leadership
item.description: The Tanzanian government is pushing strongly for environmental protection and climate change response, highlighting how these issues are central to the country's development and citizens' well-being. ...

item.title: Middle East Conflict Threatens Fertilizer Supply and US Farming
item.description: The US attack on Iran has disrupted global supply chains beyond oil, with significant consequences for American agriculture. Fertilizer markets, crucial for spring planting, are under strain as key ex...

item.title: Britney Spears arrested in California, court date set for May 4
item.description: Pop icon Britney Spears was arrested in California on Wednesday evening and booked in the early hours of Thursday, before being released at 6am. She is scheduled to appear in court on May 4. This inci...

item.title: Lone Winner from South Cotabato Claims P39.385 Million in PCSO Lotto 6/42 Draw
item.description: A fortunate bettor from South Cotabato emerged as the sole winner of the Lotto 6/42 jackpot prize amounting to P39,385,042.44 during the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) draw held on Thurs...

item.title: Understanding the Nasal Cycle and Its Role in Breathing
item.description: Even when you are healthy, you may notice that one nostril feels more open than the other when breathing. This is due to the nasal cycle, a natural process where the dominant nostril alternates multip...

item.title: GLP-1 medications may help reduce heart tissue damage after heart attacks, study finds
item.description: A recent study published in Nature Communications by the University of Bristol and University College London suggests that GLP-1 drugs, such as semaglutide, commonly used for type 2 diabetes and obesi...

item.title: Kobie Brown organizes special screening for Filipino film 'Sisa'
item.description: Kobie Brown, a prominent figure in Philippine showbiz, recently organized a block screening for the highly anticipated Filipino film 'Sisa.' The event brought together a mix of actors, filmmakers, and...

item.title: Woman sentenced for smuggling heroin and firearms into the UK
item.description: Kasha Sanderson, a 40-year-old from Bristol, has been sentenced to nine years in prison after attempting to smuggle 5kg of heroin and a collection of firearms into the United Kingdom. The incident occ...

item.title: India narrowly defeats England in T20 World Cup semi-final, advances to face New Zealand
item.description: India ek zabardast semi-final match mein England ko sirf seven runs se hara diya aur T20 World Cup ke final mein New Zealand ka saamna karegi. Mumbai ke Wankhede stadium mein khele gaye match mein San...
